page: Microsoft Azure E4d_v4 is an EDv4 family virtual machine configured with 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The underlying hardware features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz, consisting of 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. This instance includes 154 GB of local storage and does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 35 MB L3 cache, with support for AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AES instructions.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities but weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth results. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is average at 1410.79, and the Geekbench single-core score is 1234.0. However, multi-core performance is weak, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3707.88 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 2747.0. Memory bandwidth is also weak, measuring 22.87 GB/sec for large block read-only and 17.84 GB/sec for large block write operations. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show average performance, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E4d_v4 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core alongside bundled local storage, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-throughput multi-core processing. Given the weak database operations benchmark of 1382.57 and weak Redis SET performance of 3.55 million operations per second, this server is best suited for memory-optimized workloads such as caching layers, light database tasks, text processing, and basic web serving where memory capacity is prioritized over raw compute power.
description: Microsoft Azure E4d_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDv4 family, featuring 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, this x86_64 instance includes 154 GB of local storage and operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark data shows average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1234.0, but weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth results, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 3707.88. The server provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, offering qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high multi-threaded compute. It is best suited for memory-optimized applications, text processing, and light caching layers.

tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and bundled local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
